name: Orange-Sauced Chicken Stir-Fry
recipeInstructions:
1. Rinse the chicken and pat it dry. Cut into 1-inch pieces and set aside.
2. In a bowl, whisk together orange juice, cornstarch, sherry, tamari or soy sauce, and ground ginger to make the sauce.
3. Spray a cold wok or large skillet with nonstick coating.
4. Heat the wok over medium heat and add broccoli and onion. Stir-fry for 2-3 minutes until crisp-tender.
5. Remove the vegetables from the wok and set aside.
6. Add oil to the hot wok, then add chicken. Stir-fry for 2-4 minutes or until no longer pink and tender.
7. Push the chicken to one side of the wok. Pour the sauce over the chicken.
8. Cook for 2 more minutes, stirring constantly.
9. Return the vegetables to the wok and stir all ingredients together to coat with the sauce.
10. Serve immediately over cooked brown rice, garnished with orange slices.
